description_long: This train station features two symmetrical, two-story buildings with red gabled roofs and light-colored stone facades, connected by a central covered walkway. The front of the station displays arched windows and doors, with decorative stonework and green doors providing a classic, inviting appearance. The rear side is equipped with a long, covered platform canopy supported by columns, adjacent to a single railway track. The station sits on a broad concrete base, with steps and planters at the entrance, and chimneys and antennas on the roofs. The windows are large and arched, suggesting ample natural light inside, and the overall structure is robust, likely constructed from masonry and concrete. The architectural style hints at a late 19th or early 20th-century European influence, with symmetrical design and ornamental details.
description: A symmetrical, two-story train station with red gabled roofs, arched windows, and a covered platform canopy beside a single railway track. The facade features decorative stonework and green doors, evoking a classic European architectural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with arched windows, green doors, and decorative stonework on both wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the building, the red gabled roof, and the adjacent railway track running parallel to the station.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the long covered platform canopy, supporting columns, and the single railway track directly behind the station.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the building's length, roof details, and the railway track on the far side.
attribute_platform_canopies: One large, flat-roofed platform canopy at the rear, supported by multiple columns, covering the length of the platform and constructed from metal and possibly wood.
attribute_station_signage: One visible sign above the platform canopy at the rear, likely indicating the station name, centrally placed for visibility to arriving passengers.
attribute_railway_tracks: One railway track runs parallel to the rear of the station, constructed from metal rails and wooden or concrete ties, with standard spacing and no visible switches.
